{"step_by_step_thinking": "The documents provide information on the risks associated with oral contraceptives. Document [21] states that the most concerning side effects of the OC pill are venous thromboembolism (VTE) and arterial thrombosis. Document [20] also mentions that estrogen-containing contraceptives are contraindicated in patients with hypercoagulable disorders and a history of thromboembolism. Document [21] provides specific statistics showing that the rate of VTE in non-pregnant women is 4-5 per 10,000, while in OC pill users it is 9-10 in 10,000. This indicates a higher risk of thromboembolic disorders with oral contraceptive use. While breast cancer is mentioned as a risk, it is not as prominently highlighted as thromboembolic disorders in the documents. Therefore, the highest risk associated with the use of oral contraceptives is in thromboembolic disorders.", "answer_choice": "C"}
